title: Primary, secondary and tertiary education enrollment and completion rates
subtitle: This is given as the gross rate, which includes children of any age entering the level of education; this can result in percentages greater than 100 because children may enter education late or repeat a year.
caption:
Primary, secondary and tertiary education enrollment and completion rates

This is given as the gross rate, which includes children of any age entering the level of education; this can result in percentages greater than 100 because children may enter education late or repeat a year.

The chart displays five distinct trend lines tracking global education metrics from 1970 to 2024. The primary enrollment rate, represented by the top blue line, fluctuates within the upper range of the chart, generally staying between 90% and 105%. It begins near 90% in 1970, rises to a peak slightly above 100% around 2008, and ends the period just above the 100% mark. Below this, the primary completion rate, shown in green, starts around 78% in the year 2000 and follows a steady upward trajectory, reaching approximately 88% by 2024. In the middle section of the graph, the secondary enrollment rate (orange) and lower secondary completion rate (purple) show significant growth. Secondary enrollment begins near 40% in 1970, dips slightly in the early 1980s, and then climbs consistently to finish near 78% in 2024. The lower secondary completion line begins around 60% in 2000 and rises to converge with the secondary enrollment line near the 78% mark at the end of the timeline. The lowest trend line, representing tertiary enrollment in brown, starts at roughly 10% in 1970 and remains relatively flat until the mid-1990s. From that point, it exhibits a steep, continuous increase, crossing the 20% threshold around 2005 and ending the period at approximately 43%.
